purpose: The cross-client display LABELS for the post-game solver titles (the D29 award ladder; design/post-game/TITLES.md, PROTOCOL.md §12). The wire (GET /games/{id}/analysis `titles`) carries only a lowercase-kebab key and an evidence number; the label is the film-credit name a client paints for that key ("The saboteur"). These strings were client-owned prose until the server-rendered share card had to render them (native apps consume the server card PNG, not a client render; design/post-game/SHARE.md), so the labels are promoted here to shared normative ground. Frozen so web (apps/web/src/ui/titlesReadout.ts TITLE_COPY), iOS (apps/ios/Sources/CrossyUI/TitleLadder.swift), Android (apps/android/ui/src/main/kotlin/crossy/ui/TitleLadder.kt), and the server card (apps/api/src/share/titleLabels.ts) all render one title as the same words. LABELS ONLY: the evidence/detail line under a label ("Overwrote 7 correct squares") interpolates the solve's own stats and stays client-owned; it is not pinned here. Content is the web TITLE_COPY labels verbatim, the ratified card copy. Order is the engine TITLE_LADDER rank order (packages/engine/src/titles.ts), so a reader can also pin coverage; the wire already orders titles by rank, so this order is for coverage, not a sort a client applies. A client MUST still ignore an unknown key (PROTOCOL.md §12 forward compatibility): the ladder grows server-first, and a label absent here means an older client renders one fewer credit, never a crash.
